creator: michael.schuh@gmail.com
text:
2012/10/26 Josh Paetzel <josh@tcbug.org>

> On 10/19/2012 20:03, edwin@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> > Synopsis: Wrong/Missing CONFLICTS in net/openistgt
> >
> > Responsible-Changed-From-To: freebsd-ports-bugs->jpaetzel
> > Responsible-Changed-By: edwin
> > Responsible-Changed-When: Sat Oct 20 03:03:34 UTC 2012
> > Responsible-Changed-Why:
> > Over to maintainer (via the GNATS Auto Assign Tool)
> >
> > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=172892
> >
>
> It's my feeling that the openistgt port can be deleted, shortly after it
> was created work started on a kernel level iSCSI implementation.
>
>
Ok. Sounds good to me.
No idea who can decide about that, other than you.

OT: Is the actual work for the kernel level iscsi target to find in HEAD?

Many thanks.
